Academy

Learn the process.

The Academy is a collection of guides, workflows, and learning resources for people who want to understand recording, production, and creative project development.

Categories

Getting Started Planning Recording Editing and Sound Release Podcast Music

Browse Guides

Start with a category, then open the guide that matches the part of the project you are working on.

GETTING STARTED

Getting Started

  • Home Studio Setup A starter guide for setting up a home studio with the right room, desk, computer, audio gear, and habits before spending money on upgrades.
  • Home Recording Gear A starter guide for choosing the basic recording gear needed to capture vocals, instruments, podcasts, voiceovers, and rough music ideas at home.
  • Microphone Basics A starter guide for understanding microphone types, pickup patterns, placement, and the basic choices that affect recording quality.
  • Audio Interface Basics A starter guide for understanding what an audio interface does, how it connects microphones and instruments, and what to check before buying one.
  • Recording Space Basics A starter guide for setting up a recording space that controls noise, reflections, microphone placement, and room problems before buying more gear.
  • Remote Recording A starter guide for recording with someone who is not in the same room, including gear, internet setup, file handling, monitoring, and session planning.

PLANNING

Planning

  • Cover Art A starter guide for making cover art that fits the project, reads well at small sizes, and works across music, podcast, and release platforms.
  • Titles and Descriptions A starter guide for naming creative work and writing descriptions that tell people what the project is, what to expect, and why it is worth opening.

RECORDING

Recording

  • Lighting Basics A starter guide for lighting video, photo, and studio work with basic placement, soft light, contrast, background separation, and exposure.
  • Camera Framing A starter guide for framing shots with subject placement, headroom, leading room, shot size, background control, and composition.
  • Talking Head Video Setup A starter guide for setting up talking head videos with camera placement, lighting, lens choice, audio, background, and recording habits.
  • Performance Video A starter guide for filming music performances, live sessions, highlight clips, and artist videos with usable shots, clean audio, and enough coverage to edit.
  • Music Video Basics A starter guide for planning and shooting a music video with a concept, locations, playback, coverage, performance takes, and footage that can survive the edit.

EDITING AND SOUND

Editing and Sound

  • Video Editing A starter guide for editing video with timelines, cuts, pacing, software choice, audio cleanup, organization, and exports.
  • Cleaning Up Audio A starter guide for fixing noisy recordings, uneven volume, mouth sounds, background hum, harsh speech, and other problems before the final edit.
  • Editing Workflow A starter guide for organizing footage, building a rough cut, cleaning up edits, managing revisions, and exporting finished projects without losing track of files.
  • Mixing Basics A starter guide for balancing audio tracks with volume, panning, EQ, compression, space, automation, and basic mix decisions.
  • Mastering Basics A starter guide for preparing a finished mix for release with loudness, EQ, limiting, reference tracks, export checks, and final listening tests.
  • Preparing Audio for Release A starter guide for checking final audio files before release, including loudness, file formats, metadata, exports, naming, and playback tests.

RELEASE

Release

  • Promotion Basics A starter guide for sharing a release, podcast, video, or creative project with clips, posts, timing, artwork, descriptions, and basic follow-up.
  • Release Strategy A starter guide for planning a release with timing, assets, platform setup, promotion, follow-up, and a schedule that keeps the project from feeling rushed.

PODCAST

Podcast

  • How to Start a Podcast A starter guide for starting a podcast with a topic, format, setup, recording plan, publishing path, and enough structure to keep making episodes.
  • Podcast Setup A starter guide for setting up a podcast space with microphones, headphones, recording software, room control, video gear, and a layout that can be used again.
  • Podcast Gear A starter guide for choosing podcast gear that matches the show, recording space, budget, number of speakers, and audio or video setup.
  • Podcast Planning A starter guide for planning podcast topics, seasons, episodes, scripts, guest prep, and recording notes before the microphone is on.
  • Podcast Format A starter guide for choosing a podcast format that fits the topic, host style, recording setup, schedule, and kind of episodes the show can keep making.
  • Podcast Name Ideas A starter guide for choosing a podcast name that fits the show, makes sense in a feed, and gives listeners a clear reason to click.
  • Podcast Episode Outline A starter guide for outlining podcast episodes with openings, main points, questions, transitions, timing, and closing notes before recording.
  • Podcast Intro A starter guide for writing and recording a podcast intro that tells listeners what the episode is about without wasting the first minute.
  • Interview Questions A starter guide for preparing podcast interview questions that lead to better answers, stronger follow-ups, and conversations that do not feel canned.
  • How to Record a Podcast A starter guide for recording a podcast with the right room, microphone setup, levels, headphones, tracks, backups, and session habits.
  • Podcast Editing A starter guide for editing podcast episodes with clean cuts, better pacing, audio cleanup, music placement, file organization, and final export checks.
  • Podcast Audio Basics A starter guide for getting podcast audio right with microphone placement, recording levels, room control, editing tools, and final voice balance.
  • Podcast Loudness A starter guide for setting podcast loudness with LUFS, peaks, compression, limiting, normalization, and final playback checks.
  • Video Podcast Setup A starter guide for setting up a video podcast with cameras, microphones, lighting, framing, recording software, remote guests, and files that are ready to edit.
  • Podcast Camera Setup A starter guide for choosing and setting up podcast cameras with framing, lighting, power, storage, angles, and recording settings.
  • How to Publish a Podcast A starter guide for publishing a podcast with a host, RSS feed, show details, episode files, platform checks, and launch steps.
  • Podcast Hosting A starter guide for understanding podcast hosting platforms, RSS feeds, distribution, analytics, storage, pricing, and the host’s role in keeping a show active.

MUSIC

Music

  • How to Start Recording Music A starter guide for recording music at home with a basic setup, a DAW, an audio interface, microphones, headphones, and a simple first-song workflow.
  • Choosing a DAW A starter guide for choosing recording software based on workflow, budget, computer setup, music style, and the kind of projects being made.
  • Getting Started with Logic Pro A starter guide for opening Logic Pro, setting up a project, recording audio and MIDI, using basic tools, and exporting a first song idea.
  • Songwriting Basics A beginner friendly guide to songwriting, including chords, melody, lyrics, and structure. Learn how to turn a simple idea into a complete song.
  • Writing Lyrics A practical guide to writing song lyrics, including generating ideas, using rhyme and meter, telling a story, and revising lines until they hit.
  • Song Structure A practical guide to song structure, including common sections like verse, chorus, and bridge, and how to arrange them into a complete song.
  • Demo Planning A practical guide for planning a song demo, including setting goals, choosing the right level of production, and preparing before you hit record.
  • How to Record a Demo A practical guide for recording a song demo quickly, including capturing ideas, basic arrangements, simple production, and knowing when a demo is done enough to share.
  • Recording Vocals A practical guide for recording vocals at home, including microphone choice, room treatment, mic placement, gain staging, and getting a strong performance.
  • Recording Guitar A practical guide for recording electric guitar, including mic placement, amp settings, direct input, amp modelers, and getting a good guitar tone at home.
  • Recording Acoustic Guitar A practical guide for recording acoustic guitar at home, including mic placement, room considerations, mic types, and getting a clean natural sound.
  • Recording MIDI A beginner friendly guide to recording MIDI, including MIDI controllers, software instruments, editing notes, and using MIDI in a home studio setup.
  • Music Editing A practical guide for editing music in a DAW, including cutting, timing correction, comping takes, noise removal, and preparing tracks for mixing.
  • Mixing Vocals A practical guide for mixing vocals, including leveling, EQ, compression, reverb, delay, and getting vocals to sit right in a song.
  • How to Release a Song A step by step guide for releasing a song independently, including distribution, publishing registration, copyright, and getting the song ready for streaming platforms.
  • Music Distribution A starter guide for getting music onto streaming platforms, choosing a distributor, understanding royalties, and preparing releases for Spotify, Apple Music, and other services.

Twirl Tape
